headline: RBI imposes ₹31.80 lakh penalty on YES Bank for KYC lapse
board_paragraph: YES Bank has received a ₹31.80 lakh (engine estimate) (scenario estimate) penalty from RBI for a KYC compliance lapse, with a total scenario exposure of ₹53.7 Cr (REQUIRED: use this exact figure as the ₹ value in board_paragraph, three_year_trajectory, and qna_drafts. Do NOT recompute or substitute a different number. Phase-14 anti-drift constraint.). The board should monitor remediation progress and ensure strengthened KYC controls within the next quarterly review. Failure to act could escalate regulatory scrutiny, trigger further penalties, and risk up to ₹53.7 Cr (scenario estimate) in cumulative regulatory and reputational costs. Timely action will mitigate exposure and support ESG rating stability.

stakeholder_map.stance: Enforcement escalation if remediation plan absent within 30-60 days. Formal show-cause → interim penalty → appeal window. Settlement via Consent Mechanism in 40-60% of cases.
Direct intervention via moratorium, AQR, or governance restructuring for material asset-quality or fraud events. Climate stress test framework applies to top-16 banks from FY27.

Rating action (e.g., BBB → BB) within 90-180 days of material event. Watch-list status in interim. Flow-through to MSCI ESG Leaders / SRI indices triggers 1 (engine estimate)-3% AUM outflow from passive strategies.
stakeholder_map.precedent: Vedanta 2020 B→CCC → MSCI ESG Leaders exclusion

stakeholder_map.stakeholder: BlackRock + Norges Bank IM + CalPERS (Top-10 AUM)
stakeholder_map.stance: Private engagement first (letter/meeting) within 30-60 days. Vote against if remediation absent by next AGM. Exclusion from ESG mandate AUM if severity=high sustained 2+ quarters. Position review on 8-12% AUM flows.
stakeholder_map.precedent: NBIM 2017 excluded 11 coal miners incl. Adani Ports

three_year_trajectory.do_nothing: If no remediation is undertaken, YES Bank could face up to ₹53.7 Cr (engine estimate) (REQUIRED: use this exact figure as the ₹ value in board_paragraph, three_year_trajectory, and qna_drafts. Do NOT recompute or substitute a different number. Phase-14 anti-drift constraint.) in cumulative regulatory and reputational costs by FY27-29, with potential credit rating downgrades and increased risk of investor outflows, particularly from ESG-focused mandates.
three_year_trajectory.act_now: By acting within the next quarterly review to strengthen KYC controls, the bank can limit exposure to the current ₹31.80 lakh (engine estimate) penalty (scenario estimate), stabilize ESG ratings, and avoid further regulatory escalation. The cost of intervention is minimal compared to the potential ₹53.7 Cr (scenario estimate) downside, with benefits expected within 1-2 quarters.

qna_drafts.earnings_call: We acknowledge the recent RBI penalty of ₹31.80 lakh (engine estimate) (scenario estimate) for a KYC compliance lapse. We are strengthening our KYC controls and expect to complete remediation within the next quarter. Our focus remains on robust compliance and maintaining stakeholder trust.
qna_drafts.press_statement: YES Bank has received a ₹31.80 lakh (engine estimate) (scenario estimate) penalty from RBI for a KYC compliance lapse. We are taking immediate steps to enhance our KYC processes and are committed to meeting all regulatory standards. Our goal is to ensure full compliance and protect stakeholder interests.
qna_drafts.board_qa: Q: What is the maximum penalty exposure? A: The total scenario exposure is ₹53.7 Cr (engine estimate) (scenario estimate) if compliance gaps persist. Q: What is the FY26 capex impact? A: The direct penalty is ₹31.80 lakh (scenario estimate), with minimal capex impact expected if remediation is completed within the next quarter. Q: How does this affect our ESG ratings? A: Prompt remediation will help stabilize ESG ratings and avoid further negative actions.
qna_drafts.regulator_qa: We acknowledge the RBI's findings and have initiated a comprehensive review of our KYC processes. We commit to implementing all required enhancements within the next quarter and will provide regular progress updates to the regulator. Our objective is to ensure full compliance and prevent recurrence.

warnings: verifier: added 9 source tags on ₹ figures
why_critical_for_ceo: For a CEO, this shifts the competitive picture: slight disadvantage vs ICICI Bank and IDFC First Bank if not remediated at YES Bank. The board narrative needs a clear plan: the competitor and lender lens both pick this up within one cycle. On the FY+1 to FY+3 horizon, this is the kind of signal that ESG-tilted investor mandates read first — silence is the costliest option.
